title: eriogonum spergulinum subsp. reddingianum
intro: Eriogonum spergulinum subsp. reddingianum, also known as Redding buckwheat or Redding desert buckwheat, is a rare and endangered succulent plant native to California, USA. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eriogonum spergulinum subsp. reddingianum include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing and fighting off infections. Traditional herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures made from the plant's leaves and stems, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
